mechanism: Fiscal dominance, if persistent, most likely resolves through financial repression (4/7 historical base rate) rather than default (0/5 base rate) or hyperinflation (0/5 base rate). The mechanism involves sustained negative real rates, regulatory captive buying, and inflation tolerance above target.
regime_dependence: Holds for own-currency-issuing advanced economies. Historical analogues assumed capital controls and regulated banking, which may not apply under modern capital mobility. UK gilt crisis demonstrated market enforcement of real-rate discipline.
